about.title: One developer. One contact. The full stack.
about.intro: Hi, I'm André. For eight years I've been building websites and shops — as a Frontend Engineer at Germany's largest e-commerce platform by day, and as a freelancer for companies that want a reliable partner.
about.para2: No agency pipeline, no middle layer. Design, frontend, backend, SEO, hosting — I do it all myself. Which means shorter loops, transparent communication, and decisions that actually fit the project.
about.para3: I work AI-assisted. Not as a gimmick, but because it lets me ship with the care of a team in the time of one person.

work.title: Selected projects.
work.intro: A handful from recent years. Confidentiality honored on request — more references available.

items.title: Crypto banking that thinks in four languages.
items.desc: End-to-end web presence for the Swiss crypto and IBAN platform by GreatIdee AG (Zug). Multi-locale in DE, EN, FR and IT with App Store CTAs as the central conversion path, on top of a regulation-heavy content stack (SO-FIT, upcoming MiCA, AML/KYC). A custom WordPress theme — no page builder — paired with ACF Flexible Content delivers a block library that the client rearranges via drag-and-drop and reuses across pages. Ongoing technical maintenance and iteration since launch in 2024.

screenshots.alt: Get-started section: three steps to the app
screenshots.caption: Get-started section: the path to the app in three steps.

screenshots.alt: Live crypto market table with currency and date filter
screenshots.caption: Live crypto market: real-time prices, currency switcher and date filter — a custom shortcode the marketing team drops in wherever needed.
Feature section: one of 41 reusable ACF Flexible Content blocks.

screenshots.alt: Services section with four service cards
screenshots.caption: Services section: reordered via drag-and-drop, no developer needed.

case.challenge: A multilingual, heavily regulated fintech platform needs content the marketing team owns end-to-end — down to the order of sections on every page. Off-the-shelf page builders like Elementor or Divi cost performance, lock you out of markup control, and produce HTML a team struggles to maintain or migrate later. On top: geo-specific content (country availability, regulatory disclaimers) and four language versions (DE, EN, FR, IT), each covering dozens of pages.
case.solution: Custom Underscores-based theme instead of a page builder — full control over markup, CSS, and JS. ACF Flexible Content delivers a block library of 41 reusable section types (hero variants, feature cards, FAQ, banners, country grid, and more) that the client recombines and reorders on any page via drag-and-drop. For dynamic content — supported/non-supported countries, money transfer calculator — custom shortcodes let the marketing team drop interactive widgets into any text block. SCSS build pipeline with Stylelint and RTL support, separate desktop and mobile header templates for clean per-device navigation, multilingual architecture via WordPress gettext.
case.outcome: Launched 2024, ongoing technical maintenance and iteration since. The marketing team builds new landing pages independently from the block library — section order, content, and geo availability are all editable without involving a developer. Even global elements like header navigation, footer links, and social profiles are managed via dedicated ACF Options Pages. Scaled from launch to 30+ pages across 4 languages without restructuring the underlying CMS architecture.

items.type: Platform · LMS · Affiliate System · Digistore Integration
items.title: Courses, access, commissions — a learning platform that runs itself.
items.desc: Online learning platform for premium courses (e-commerce, dropshipping, AI, social media, UGC). Sales run through Digistore24 — which offers no interface for automatic course provisioning or a multi-tier partner commission model. So the entire integration layer was built from scratch: a WordPress REST webhook receives every Digistore event (purchase, refund, chargeback), creates accounts, unlocks the right MasterStudy courses, books affiliate commissions across multiple tiers, and sends the matching emails — idempotent, with automatic clawback on refund. Plus custom admin tools for commission rates and email management.

screenshots.alt: Course catalogue “Wähle deinen Weg” with six premium courses and prices
screenshots.caption: Course catalogue: each course is a Digistore product. A product-ID mapping tells the platform which MasterStudy courses to unlock after purchase — bundles included.

screenshots.alt: Course detail page “Werde Prime Partner” with Digistore buy button and programme description
screenshots.caption: Course page with a Digistore checkout button. The referring partner from the tracking cookie is appended to the checkout URL via JavaScript, so the sale is later attributed to the correct commission tier.

screenshots.alt: Course player for the e-commerce lesson with curriculum sidebar, video and progress bar
screenshots.caption: The learning environment on MasterStudy LMS: curriculum, video lessons, progress tracking. Access is granted only after a verified Digistore purchase.

screenshots.alt: Partner area with personal referral link and multi-tier network visualisation
screenshots.caption: The custom-built partner area: personal referral link and a multi-tier network. Commissions cascade up the partner hierarchy — with cycle protection when linking and clawback on refund.

case.challenge: Profit Prime sells premium online courses through Digistore24 — payment, invoicing, VAT, all outsourced. The catch: Digistore offers no interface to trigger the right things automatically after a purchase. And a lot needs to happen. The buyer needs an account and access to exactly the courses they bought, instantly. At the same time a multi-tier partner programme runs: whoever refers a buyer earns a commission — and whoever referred that partner earns too, across multiple tiers. Refunds and chargebacks have to revoke access again and reverse commissions already credited. None of this is manageable by hand during live sales, and no off-the-shelf plugin fully covers the Digistore integration together with the multi-tier commission logic.
case.solution: A fully custom integration layer on WordPress that connects Digistore to the learning platform. At its core is a dedicated REST webhook endpoint that receives and orchestrates every Digistore IPN event. On a purchase it creates a WordPress account if needed (passwordless: the user gets a set-password link rather than a plaintext password), unlocks the matching MasterStudy LMS courses via a product-ID-to-course mapping (bundles included), with membership levels running in parallel through Paid Memberships Pro. The affiliate system builds on AffiliateWP and its Multi-Tier add-on but is orchestrated by custom code: the referral is booked with the right type so the commission cascade fires cleanly across partner tiers, custom progressive commission rates are applied (freely configurable in the admin), and a cycle check prevents infinite loops when building the partner hierarchy. On refund, chargeback or missed payment the same endpoint revokes course access and marks the commission as “rejected” (clawback). Every transactional email — access, course unlock, partner welcome, commission credit — is in German and idempotent. Plus custom admin pages: commission rates, a tool to re-send individual emails, and granular notification settings. The pages themselves are built with Hello Elementor + Elementor Pro — all the business logic lives in the theme code behind them.
case.outcome: Live since 2026. Every sale through Digistore runs fully automatically: account, course unlock, commission distribution across all tiers, and email delivery all happen within seconds of purchase, with no manual step. The integration is built for continuous operation — Digistore's repeated IPN calls are processed idempotently with multiple safeguards (no duplicate account, no duplicate course, no duplicate commission), and every step is written to an access-protected log that triggers an admin notification on failure. Refunds and chargebacks pull access and commissions back automatically. Commission rates and notifications are all adjustable without touching code via the custom admin pages. The result: a self-built backend carrying a business model the market had no off-the-shelf solution for — from the first payment to a third-tier commission.

items.title: A beauty studio that runs its own website end-to-end.
items.desc: Beauty institute in Oldenburg, part of the Augusta-Praxis family. Custom WordPress theme on Underscores instead of a page builder — but with page-builder convenience via ACF Flexible Content: 16 reusable section types the studio team combines, reorders, and fills with content themselves. Dynamic theming via ACF Options Pages, custom contact form handler with hCaptcha. Live since 2023, ongoing maintenance.

screenshots.alt: Team intro and content-card grid with treatment teasers
screenshots.caption: Team section and content cards: two different ACF blocks, arranged by the studio in the WP backend as needed.

screenshots.alt: Service spectrum: four-column grid with treatment lists
screenshots.caption: Service-spectrum section from the ACF service-grid block — columns and lists fully maintained by the studio.

screenshots.alt: Contact form with hCaptcha and footer
screenshots.caption: Contact form with a bespoke PHP handler: HTML5 client-side validation, server-side hCaptcha verification, dual HTML mail dispatch (studio notification plus customer auto-reply).

case.challenge: A beauty institute in Oldenburg — part of the Augusta-Praxis clinic family — needed a website the studio team could fully maintain themselves: update treatment descriptions, swap seasonal hero images, add service packages, reorder sections, all without a developer. Off-the-shelf salon templates feel interchangeable. Page builders like Elementor offer self-service but cost performance (JS bloat, lots of asset requests) and limit design freedom (rigid widget APIs, specificity battles for custom styling). Those limits had already shown up on the clinic group's previous project. On top of that: GDPR-compliant contact forms (no reCAPTCHA), WhatsApp and appointment booking as primary conversion paths, and dynamic branding adjustable without touching code.
case.solution: Custom theme on Underscores — not a page builder, but page-builder convenience via ACF Flexible Content. The studio gets its own block library of 16 reusable section types, re-arrangeable through the WP admin UI and fillable with its own content: hero carousel, service grid, content cards, Swiper-9 image slider, testimonial carousel, text-video box (YouTube, self-hosted, Instagram embed), banner, contact section, accordion, grid layouts. The team maintains content and section order itself; small design changes (primary/secondary color, logo, footer content, social links) flow through dedicated ACF Options Pages — colors are injected as CSS custom properties straight into the header, so a brand-switch needs no developer. Deliberately built as a reusable platform. Custom contact form handler in functions.php: HTML5 client-side validation, server-side hCaptcha verification, dual HTML mail dispatch (studio notification plus customer auto-reply). Bootstrap 5 grid plus Swiper 9 carousel, RTL-prepared SCSS pipeline via node-sass, custom German breadcrumb navigation, integrated WhatsApp direct link.
case.outcome: Launched 2023, with ongoing technical maintenance since. The studio team maintains all content independently — treatment descriptions, seasonal hero carousels, team introductions — via the ACF section library. The five service categories (Kosmetik⁺, HydraFacial, Sugaring, Nail Couture, Lashes & Brows) are each composed from the same block library, each with their own mega-menu entry. Contact form, direct WhatsApp link, and appointment-booking buttons offer parallel conversion paths. The theme foundation has since been reused for the clinic group's next project — same block library, same build pipeline, same form handler, with its own branding (colors, logo, content). Core updates flow into both sites simultaneously.

items.type: Website · Multilingual · WordPress · Custom CMS · Maintenance
items.title: A liposuction institute in two languages — same theme, new context.
items.desc: Liposuction and lipedema institute in Oldenburg, part of the Augusta-Praxis family. Built on the same custom-theme foundation as augustabeauty.de — with three targeted extensions for the clinical context: accordion block added, DE/EN multilingual layer in the header, clinical branding toggled via ACF Options Pages. Shows the theme platform grows with the client rather than staying frozen.

screenshots.alt: Service spectrum: four treatment categories as image cards
screenshots.caption: Service-spectrum section from the ACF service-grid block — same pattern as on augustabeauty.de, re-composed for the medical context.

screenshots.alt: Patient-guide section with two specialist books on lipedema
screenshots.caption: Patient guides: bibliographic content with ISBN data as an ACF content block — maintained by the clinic team, no developer needed.

screenshots.alt: Contact form with hCaptcha and footer cross-links to sister sites
screenshots.caption: Footer cross-links to the sister sites of the Augusta-Praxis family — augustapraxis.de, augustabeauty.de, pigmentfrei.de, augenlidchirurgie.com.

case.challenge: After Augusta Beauty, the next project in the Augusta-Praxis family came up: a dedicated web presence for the Lipold institute — specialists for liposuction and lipedema treatment. Different audience than the cosmetic sister brand: clinical-medical context, longer patient journeys, more trust-building content. Plus a second language (English) for international patients, stage-specific treatment descriptions, anatomical service categories, patient testimonials, patient-guide books, multiple specialists. Platform requirement: the same content-management comfort as on augustabeauty.de — arrange sections yourself, maintain content yourself — but now bilingual and with additional section types the Augusta theme didn't yet have, foremost a FAQ accordion for stage explanations and treatment-flow steps.
case.solution: Augusta theme platform as the foundation — same 16-block library as starting point, same build pipeline, same contact form handler. Three targeted extensions for the Lipold context: (1) accordion block added for FAQ sections, stage explanations, and treatment-flow steps — 17 blocks total. (2) DE/EN multilingual layer in the header: language switcher, translated mega-menus, localized form labels. (3) Clinical branding: teal primary instead of charcoal, same gold secondary as Augusta — toggled exclusively via ACF Options Pages, no code changes. Content is maintained by the clinic team through the same WP admin UI as on augustabeauty.de. Footer cross-links to the sister sites of the Augusta-Praxis family. Evidence the theme foundation matures: same comfort guarantee for the editorial team, new capabilities for the use case.
case.outcome: Live since 2024. The clinic team maintains bilingual content independently — treatment descriptions, stage explanations, patient-guide listings, testimonials — via the ACF section library. Treatment categories (lipedema, liposuction, skin tightening, and more) are composed from the same block library. The theme foundation now runs on two Augusta-Praxis sites in parallel: core updates are maintained centrally, form handler and SCSS pipeline shared. The accordion block extension stays in the Lipold repository but is written so it can be ported back into the Augusta theme whenever FAQ sections are needed there.

items.title: Where Elementor was enough — and where the platform story began.
items.desc: Tattoo-laser and PMU-removal centre in Oldenburg, the first web identity of the Augusta-Praxis group. Deliberately built on Hello Elementor + Elementor Pro: narrow scope, focused landing page, time-to-launch was the priority. The experience with Elementor's performance footprint and design limits shaped the move to a custom theme for the group's next projects (Augusta Beauty, Lipold).

screenshots.alt: Picosecond-laser section with device photo and technical description
screenshots.caption: Picosecond-laser section: tech detail block with device photo and description — assembled from Elementor Pro widgets, custom CSS for the Augusta-Praxis brand colors.

screenshots.alt: Treatment process as a six-step accordion
screenshots.caption: Treatment process as an Elementor accordion (6 steps). This is precisely the use case that later inspired the accordion block extension in the custom theme on the sister site Lipold.
Testimonial carousel and contact form — both built as Elementor Pro widgets, in the Augusta-Praxis brand look.

case.challenge: The Augusta-Praxis group needed its first own web identity for its tattoo-laser and PMU-removal service. Scope: a focused landing-page site, clear conversion paths (consultation booking, WhatsApp, phone), self-service maintenance by the clinic team. Time-to-launch was important — market entry shouldn't wait. No multi-locale, no complex multi-page structure required at this point.
case.solution: Hello Elementor as parent theme + Elementor Pro. Quick implementation via the Elementor block library: hero carousel, feature sections, accordion for the treatment process, testimonial carousel, price list, contact form. Custom CSS for the Augusta-Praxis brand colors (rose-gold accents, soft beige tones). The clinic team maintains content directly through the Elementor UI — move sections, update text, add new FAQ items or prices.
case.outcome: Live since 2023, the clinic team maintains the site independently. It does its job for the defined scope. During day-to-day operation two limits became visible that shaped later stack decisions: (1) Performance footprint — Elementor + Elementor Pro bring noticeable JS, asset requests, and DOM complexity, which is tolerable for a single landing page but would get expensive on a multi-page architecture with five service categories. (2) Design limits — Elementor's widget API is rigid; unusual layouts (e.g. a dynamic DE/EN language switcher, a fully customisable multi-email form handler with hCaptcha) are only achievable via custom widget plugins or CSS specificity battles. From those experiences came the move, for the group's next projects (Augusta Beauty 2023, Lipold 2024), to a custom theme on Underscores + ACF Flexible Content — same self-service comfort for the clinic team, without the page-builder costs. Pigmentfrei keeps running on Elementor: migration would be possible but isn't strictly necessary for the current scope.

faq.a: Usually 100 % up front. For larger projects, 50 / 50 on request — half to start, half at launch. Invoiced with short payment terms, by bank transfer.

faq.q: Do we have to get on a call?
faq.a: No. If you'd like, we'll hop on a quick call — but email works just as well. Whatever suits you.

faq.q: How long does a project take?
faq.a: Very flexible — from a few days to a few weeks, depending on scope, payment and my availability. With a clear scope and some urgency, overnight is possible too. Exact timeline after a quick chat.

Yes, throughout. AI is a core part of my workflow — from research to code to testing. It makes me faster and therefore cheaper, with no compromise on quality, security or maintainability. I review and own the code myself.
Yes. Once paid, everything is yours — code, design, content. No vendor lock-in, no forced subscription. Host and maintain it yourself, or keep me on.
Domain and hosting fees, licenses for premium plugins or stock photography, and ongoing maintenance after the included support window.
Some support included depending on the project. After that, an optional maintenance retainer or hourly billing — whatever you need.

products.title: WordPress plugins I build and maintain myself.
products.intro: Focused, well-maintained plugins for the German market — built by a senior developer, not a plugin factory. Honest tools instead of feature graveyards.

items.name: Barrierefrei Check
items.tagline: BFSG & WCAG compliance for WordPress — honest audit instead of an overlay.

detail.lede: Check your website for accessibility compliance with WCAG 2.2 and BFSG in minutes, fix the most critical issues with concrete guidance, and generate a legally sound accessibility statement. No overlay widget that merely masks problems — a real audit of your rendered HTML.
detail.problem: Since 28 June 2025, the German Accessibility Strengthening Act (BFSG) requires many online shops and service providers to offer accessible websites — violations can result in legal warnings. Most one-click overlay tools do not meet the requirements: they mask problems via JavaScript instead of solving them, and are rejected by courts and disability advocacy groups.
detail.solution: Barrierefrei Check analyses the real, rendered HTML of your pages — exactly as screen readers and assistive technologies see it. You get a prioritised list of concrete issues by severity, each with the corresponding WCAG criterion and a plain-language fix guide. On top of that, you can generate a BFSG-compliant accessibility statement via a simple form.

features.title: 12 automated audit rules
features.desc: WCAG 2.2 / BFSG: alt texts, form labels, heading structure, landmarks, language attributes, link text and more.

features.title: Real audit, no overlay
features.desc: Analyses your rendered HTML instead of masking problems with JavaScript. Honest results that hold up in court.

features.title: Prioritised by severity
features.desc: Critical, serious, moderate, minor — you see immediately what needs fixing first.

features.title: Concrete fix guidance
features.desc: Every issue comes with a plain-language guide and the exact WCAG reference — no jargon guessing game.

features.title: Statement generator
features.desc: Generate a BFSG-compliant accessibility statement as a WordPress page via a simple form — with conformance status, contact, and arbitration body.

features.title: German, for the German market
features.desc: Fully in German and aligned with BFSG / EU Directive 2019/882 — not a translated US solution.

detail.pricingNote: Prices incl. German VAT · annual license. Pro is launching soon — secure early-access pricing now.

faq.q: Does this plugin make my website automatically accessible?
faq.a: No — and no one can honestly promise that. Automated tests catch a portion of WCAG criteria. Barrierefrei Check shows you exactly what needs doing and helps you fix it. Colour contrast, keyboard navigation and content require additional manual review.
The Barrierefreiheitsstärkungsgesetz implements the EU Directive 2019/882 (European Accessibility Act) in Germany. Since 28 June 2025, many B2C online shops and service providers must be accessible.

faq.q: How is it different from overlay tools like UserWay or accessiBe?
faq.a: Overlays place a JavaScript layer over your page and mask problems instead of solving them — they are rejected by disability advocacy groups and in court proceedings. Barrierefrei Check audits the actual source code and helps you fix the root causes.

Not for the audit or the accessibility statement. Fixing some issues is easier with basic HTML knowledge and familiarity with your theme — but the guides are written so that editors can handle most fixes themselves.
